  "findings": [
    "Schrader valve cores were left in place during the evacuation process, severely restricting flow and preventing a deep, timely vacuum.",
    "Potential damage to the Schrader core rubber seals due to heat if they were also present during brazing."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Recover refrigerant from the system.",
    "Replace the potentially damaged Schrader valve cores.",
    "Evacuate the system properly to under 500 microns with cores removed using a vacuum rated core removal tool.",
    "Reinstall cores under vacuum, leak test, and recharge system to manufacturer specifications."
  ],
  "rationale": "Leaving Schrader cores in during evacuation severely restricts the flow rate, making it extremely difficult to pull a true deep vacuum (below 500 microns) and effectively remove moisture and non-condensables from the system. This leads to degraded system performance, acid formation, and premature compressor failure.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "ACCA Quality Installation Standard",
    "OEM Evacuation Guidelines"
  ]
}
